euler_problems: Problem 521 - Smallest Prime Factor: Let smpf(n) be the smallest prime factor of n. Let S(n) = sum of smpf(i) for 2 <= i <= n. Find S(10^12) mod 10^9.
explanation: The code defines three different functions, each employing bit manipulation to determine and sum the number of prime multiples up to a defined limit (10^12). The main function calls one of these implementations to execute the calculation and display the total sum of prime multipliers. Each function modifies a bitset to track prime numbers, accounting for pairs of values to optimize memory usage.
name: 2015-07-30_euler-problem-521
subject: euler Problem 521
summary: This project implements a solution to Project Euler Problem 521 using bitsets to efficiently compute prime numbers up to a large limit.
